prompt: I have a Cache interface with get(key): T and put(key, value): Unit. My BoundedCache implementation throws IllegalStateException from put() when the cache is full instead of evicting. Is this a SOLID violation, and if so which one?
rubric: Explains precondition-strengthening: BoundedCache adds a precondition (cache not full) that Cache callers don't expect
Mentions that callers would need `instanceof`/`is BoundedCache` branches — the smell LSP prevents
Suggests fixing by either making put's contract explicit (return bool / evicted entry / documented eviction) OR splitting interfaces (Cache vs BoundedCache with different put semantics) per ISP
Does NOT claim this is purely an SRP or OCP problem
